Off Calendar May-02-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion To Correct Clerical Mistake In Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to correct clerical mistake in judgment" is granted.

Order signed. Amended judgment signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2020 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-28-2020 Continued To Jul-28-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing Of Default Prove Up
Off Calendar Jun-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Fercan Enterprises, LLC and Raiza Faith Ercan.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Marc T. Cefaula, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff Bank of the West.
No appearance by defendants Fercan Enterprises, LLC and Raiza Faith Ercan.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Breach of Guaranty, Account Stated, etc. was filed on July 9, 2018, and personally served on defendants Fercan Enterprises, LLC and Raiza Faith Ercan on September 28, 2018.
Default as to defendants Fercan Enterprises, LLC and Raiza Faith Ercan was entered on November 27, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on January 28, 2020.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed today, June 2, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bank of the West, and against the defaulted defendants Fercan Enterprises, LLC and Raiza Faith Ercan, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $93,445.40. (Amount Pled in Complaint)
Attorney Fees - $2,000.00.
Costs - $866.97.

Total - $96,312.37.

Additionally, plaintiff Bank of the West is entitled to possession of the equipment identified in Exhibit A attached to the Judgment. If the equipment is recovered and sold, this Judgment will be reduced by such net sales proceeds.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
